# Compute Spot Index

URL: https://chiragmirani.github.io/compute-spot-index/

Compute Spot Index is a public AI compute price tracker. It summarizes public Vast.ai rental offers into median prices per graphics processor-hour, then translates those prices into rough annual gross value and estimated present value for a 100,000-processor AI data center.

Primary data:
- Public Vast.ai rental offers, normalized to price per graphics processor-hour.
- Saved public snapshot: https://chiragmirani.github.io/compute-spot-index/data/latest-public.json

Core formula:
- Annual gross value = median hourly rental price * 100,000 graphics processors * 8,760 hours * 65% utilization.
- Estimated present value uses 10 years and a 10% to 15% discount rate.

Reference context:
- Epoch AI, "Frontier Data Centers". Published online at epoch.ai. Retrieved from "https://epoch.ai/data/data-centers" [online resource].
- Epoch AI, "We estimate the cost breakdown for 60% of the total cost of ownership of a one-gigawatt AI data center". Published online at epoch.ai. Retrieved from "https://epoch.ai/data-insights/ai-datacenter-cost-breakdown" [online resource].

Caveat:
- These are public spot-market prices, not hyperscaler procurement costs or net operating margins. The estimates exclude electricity, maintenance, financing, networking, downtime, taxes, hardware replacement, and the risk that rental prices fall as supply expands.
